pdg_node.statement: (sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> tuple[positive int, positive int, float (seconds), float]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Compute all scalar parameters needed for the FSM detector: the moving-window length N, the delay Nd, the minimum refractory period Rmin, and pre-allocate the running amplitude accumulator Ramptotal.

pdg_node.statement: (signal: 1-D numeric array, Nd: positive int) -> 1-D float array
pdg_node.informal_desc: Apply a double finite-difference operator to the ECG signal (first a 1-step difference on the Nd-delayed signal, then a 1-step difference on that result) and square each sample to produce a non-negative energy signal.

declaration.name: compute_double_difference_squared
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D numeric array, Nd: positive int) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Apply a double finite-difference operator to the ECG signal (first a 1-step difference on the Nd-delayed signal, then a 1-step difference on that result) and square each sample to produce a non-negative energy signal.

declaration.name: compute_double_difference_squared
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D numeric array, Nd: positive int) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Apply a double finite-difference operator to the ECG signal (first a 1-step difference on the Nd-delayed signal, then a 1-step difference on that result) and square each sample to produce a non-negative energy signal.

declaration.name: compute_double_difference_squared
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D numeric array, Nd: positive int) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Apply a double finite-difference operator to the ECG signal (first a 1-step difference on the Nd-delayed signal, then a 1-step difference on that result) and square each sample to produce a non-negative energy signal.

pdg_node.statement: (squar: 1-D float array, N: positive int) -> 1-D float array
pdg_node.informal_desc: Smooth the squared double-difference signal by convolving it with a rectangular (boxcar) window of length N via a causal IIR/FIR filter, producing an integrated energy envelope used for threshold comparisons.

declaration.name: integrate_with_moving_window
declaration.type_signature: (squar: 1-D float array, N: positive int) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Smooth the squared double-difference signal by convolving it with a rectangular (boxcar) window of length N via a causal IIR/FIR filter, producing an integrated energy envelope used for threshold comparisons.

declaration.name: integrate_with_moving_window
declaration.type_signature: (squar: 1-D float array, N: positive int) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Smooth the squared double-difference signal by convolving it with a rectangular (boxcar) window of length N via a causal IIR/FIR filter, producing an integrated energy envelope used for threshold comparisons.

declaration.name: integrate_with_moving_window
declaration.type_signature: (squar: 1-D float array, N: positive int) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Smooth the squared double-difference signal by convolving it with a rectangular (boxcar) window of length N via a causal IIR/FIR filter, producing an integrated energy envelope used for threshold comparisons.

pdg_node.statement: (processed_ecg: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: positive float, Rmin: float, Ramptotal: float, Pth: positive float) -> list of int
pdg_node.informal_desc: Run the three-state finite-state machine over the integrated energy envelope to locate R-peaks: (1) scan a Rmin-wide window for the local maximum (candidate R-peak), (2) enforce a 200 ms waiting/refractory period, (3) apply an exponentially-decaying adaptive threshold to find the next above-threshold crossing, updating the running amplitude estimate Ramptotal after each detected peak.

declaration.name: detect_rpeaks_with_adaptive_threshold_fsm
declaration.type_signature: (processed_ecg: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: positive float, Rmin: float, Ramptotal: float, Pth: positive float) -> list of int
declaration.docstring: Run the three-state finite-state machine over the integrated energy envelope to locate R-peaks: (1) scan a Rmin-wide window for the local maximum (candidate R-peak), (2) enforce a 200 ms waiting/refractory period, (3) apply an exponentially-decaying adaptive threshold to find the next above-threshold crossing, updating the running amplitude estimate Ramptotal after each detected peak.

declaration.name: detect_rpeaks_with_adaptive_threshold_fsm
declaration.type_signature: (processed_ecg: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: positive float, Rmin: float, Ramptotal: float, Pth: positive float) -> list of int
declaration.docstring: Run the three-state finite-state machine over the integrated energy envelope to locate R-peaks: (1) scan a Rmin-wide window for the local maximum (candidate R-peak), (2) enforce a 200 ms waiting/refractory period, (3) apply an exponentially-decaying adaptive threshold to find the next above-threshold crossing, updating the running amplitude estimate Ramptotal after each detected peak.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Wrap the raw R-peak index list into the library's standard named-tuple return structure (ReturnTuple) so the output is consistent with the rest of the biosppy API.
